body:
## Summary

Add claude skill specific to review the Mythos PR code review

<!-- Brief description of what this PR does and why it's needed -->
<!-- Can be taken from the Copilot summary -->

## How to Test

<!-- Provide clear steps for reviewers to test your changes -->

Run `/review-mythos-pr feature/MT-293-billing-pipe 88 293` inside Claude Code

## Related Issues

<!-- Link related issues using: Closes #123, Fixes #456, Related to #789 -->

None, initiative-based

## Author Checklist

<!-- Set items as strikethrough if they are not necessary  -->

- [ ] Code follows team coding standards and style guide
- [ ] Self-reviewed the code changes
- [ ] Added/updated tests for new functionality
- [ ] All tests pass locally
- [ ] Code is properly documented
- [ ] Synced with latest `main` branch
- [ ] PR title follows conventional commit format
- [ ] Meaningful commit messages used
- [ ] Add AI Feedback Author comment (after PR is merged)

```
AI Feedback: Author
[numbering] TP/FP | (Impact) Suggestion | author notes

model: model-1, model-2, ...
note: -
```

**Example:**
```
AI Feedback: Author
1. TP | (High) Consider refactoring the data access layer for better performance | Fixed as suggested.
2. FP | (Low) Minor typo in variable naming | False positive, naming follows existing conventions.
3. FP | (High) Replace invalid `getFirst()` call | The method is valid for the Python version we use.

model: gpt-4, claude-sonnet-4
note: AI reviewer suggested improvements that improved code quality. Some suggestions were false positives due to version differences.
```

**Legend:**
- `TP`: True Positive (valid suggestion)
- `FP`: False Positive (invalid suggestion)
- `High`, `Medium`, `Low`: Impact level of the suggestion
- `note`: Action taken, lessons learned, or reason for disagreement

## Reviewer Checklist

- [ ] Comment `START REVIEW` before reviewing, once per PR
  - Review timing is measured from "START REVIEW" to last activity before merge
- [ ] Add AI Feedback Reviewer comment (after PR is merged)

```
AI Feedback: Reviewer
model: model-1, model-2, ...
note: -
```

**Example:**
```
AI Feedback: Reviewer
model: copilot(auto), claude-sonnet-4
note: AI helped identify potential race condition in async handler. AI suggested more efficient algorithm for data processing that improved performance by 30%.
```

**Legend:**
- `model`: AI models used during review (e.g., copilot, claude, gpt-4)
- `note`: Lessons learned and improvement suggestions summary
title: feat: Add review skills for easier review process

body:
## Summary

Introduces a new `features/auth` vertical slice and updates the **Login** and **Creator Apply (Step 1 — Basic Profile)** experiences to match the current Figma Creator Studio designs. This PR reflects the **current** state of the login/signup/creator-registration flow; additional commits will follow as designs are finalized.

**Note:** This branch is based on recent listing-wizard work. If you prefer a auth-only diff, we can rebase onto `main` before merge.

---

## What's in this change

### Login (`/login`)
- Dark Creator Studio layout per Figma (backdrop `#1a1a1a`, card `#131313`, fields/OAuth `#0f0f0f`)
- Email/password sign-in via existing `/api/auth/credentials-signin` + NextAuth credentials session
- Remember me (email persisted in `localStorage`)
- Google OAuth (`signIn('google')`)
- GitHub button present but **disabled** ("Coming soon") — no provider wired yet
- Footer link: **Create creator account** → `/creator/apply`
- No MUI on login; shadcn/Radix + Tailwind v4 + Space Grotesk

### Creator apply — Step 1 (`/creator/apply`)
- Figma-aligned shell: progress header ("Apply to be a **creator**", step 1/6 bar), Basic Profile form
- Fields: legal name, phone, verified read-only email, country combobox, address, street/city, postal code
- Zod validation + React Hook Form for Step 1; Back disabled on step 1, Next advances to Step 2
- Steps 2–6 retain existing logic (MUI in step bodies); wrapped in new dark auth chrome

### Routing & access
- `/creator/apply` is **public** so logged-out users can start registration from login
- `callbackUrl` supported after login (e.g. return to apply)
- Creator status API only fetched when authenticated

### Architecture
- New module: `src/features/auth/` (components, hooks, schemas, tokens)
- Thin route files: `src/app/login/page.tsx`, `src/app/creator/apply/page.tsx`

---

## Design / product note

This matches the **current** MVP wireframes, not a final sign-off. Expect follow-up PRs for:

- Remaining creator apply steps (2–6) restyled to Figma (remove legacy MUI)
- GitHub OAuth when backend/NextAuth provider is ready
- Signup/onboarding alignment with the same auth design system
- Pixel polish, responsive breakpoints, and any token updates when design is locked

---

## Figma references

- Login: https://www.figma.com/design/tunwgaAZ5f1BDI9gwE77OI/MVP-wireframes?node-id=9965-2912
- Creator apply Step 1: https://www.figma.com/design/tunwgaAZ5f1BDI9gwE77OI/MVP-wireframes?node-id=9967-3507

---

## Test plan

- [ ] `/login` — layout/colors match spec; email/password validation; Remember me persists email
- [ ] Sign in with credentials → discovery (verified) or `/verify` (unverified)
- [ ] Google sign-in starts OAuth flow
- [ ] GitHub button visible, disabled, no action
- [ ] **Create creator account** (logged out) → `/creator/apply` Step 1 (no redirect loop to login)
- [ ] Step 1 — required fields, country dropdown, verified email when logged in
- [ ] Step 1 — Back disabled; Next validates and opens Step 2
- [ ] `/login?callbackUrl=/creator/apply` — after login, lands on apply page
- [ ] Responsive check on mobile width
title: feat(auth): Login and Creator Application

body:
## Summary

- Replaces the existing MUI `'use client'` discovery page with a dark-themed RSC
  implementation built from the correct Figma frame (node 9682:3884)
- Filter bar matches design: All / Tools / APIs / Templates / AI Agents / Bundles / Resources
- Three distinct sections: Recommended horizontal scroll, Trending 2-card banner, Explore All grid
- Filter state lives entirely in the URL (`?type=…`) — no Zustand, no Context
- Mock API route added so future consumers can hit a typed endpoint immediately
- No MUI in new code — Tailwind CSS v4 throughout

---

## New Mock API Route (`src/app/api/mock/`)

| Route | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `GET /api/mock/listings?type=…` | Returns `{ listings, total }` filtered by `ListingType`; `type=all` returns full set |

## New Feature Slice (`src/features/discovery/`)

- **`types/index.ts`** — `ListingType` (`tools` / `apis` / `templates` / `ai_agents` / `bundles` / `resources`),
  `ListingBadge`, `DiscoveryListing` interfaces
- **`mock/listings.ts`** — 12 mock listings across all types; exports `MOCK_LISTINGS`,
  `RECOMMENDED_LISTINGS` (first 5), `TRENDING_BANNER_LISTINGS` (2 featured picks)
- **`discovery-card.tsx`** — Gradient thumbnail with grid overlay, badge pill (Hot / New /
  Featured / Trending), star rating, review count, price; links to `/listing/{id}`
- **`discovery-hero.tsx`** — "Discover the future of development." heading, subtitle, dual CTAs
  (Start Exploring + About us), Live throughput stat card with bar-chart visualisation,
  Active agents badge
- **`discovery-filters.tsx`** — `'use client'`; `useRouter` + `useSearchParams` to push
  `?type=…`; 7 filter pills matching the design
- **`discovery-section.tsx`** — Three exported components:
  - `RecommendedSection` — labelled horizontal scroll row with View all link
  - `TrendingBanner` — full-width dark card with "Discover Next-Gen AI Tools…" heading
    and 2-column wide-card layout
  - `ExploreAllSection` — 4-column card grid with listing count

## App Layer

- `src/app/discovery/page.tsx` — RSC; reads `searchParams.type`; Recommended and Trending
  sections hidden when a filter is active; wraps `<DiscoveryFilters>` in `<Suspense>`;
  replaces old MUI client component entirely

---

## Test Plan

- [ ] Visit `/discovery` — hero, filter bar, Recommended scroll, Trending banner, and
      Explore All grid all render
- [ ] Click "Tools" filter — URL updates to `?type=tools`, Recommended and Trending
      sections hide, grid shows only tool listings
- [ ] Click "All" — sections reappear, full grid restored
- [ ] Click a listing card — navigates to `/listing/{id}`
- [ ] `GET /api/mock/listings?type=apis` — returns only API listings
- [ ] `GET /api/mock/listings` — returns all 12 listings
title: feat(discovery): add Discovery landing page

body:
## Summary

- Implements the About marketing page at `/about` as a full RSC feature slice
- All sections are server components; only the waitlist form is `'use client'`
- Marquee ticker is pure CSS — no JS dependency, no library
- No MUI in new code — Tailwind CSS v4 throughout

---

## New Feature Slice (`src/features/about/`)

- **`about-hero.tsx`** — Announcement pill with glow dot, "Build faster. / Ship smarter."
  heading with gradient text, dual CTAs (orange primary + glassmorphism ghost), stat row
  (2 creators · 0 infra changes · 12M+ API calls), metric cards + terminal code preview
- **`about-ticker.tsx`** — Infinite horizontal marquee of 8 platform value props; items
  duplicated for seamless loop; JetBrains Mono; driven by `.animate-marquee` CSS class
- **`about-platform.tsx`** — 7-card `grid-cols-3` feature grid with asymmetric layout;
  wide `col-span-2` cards for "Auth, handled" and "SOC 2 by default"; all icons inline SVG
- **`about-waitlist.tsx`** — Controlled email input; on submit sets local `submitted` state
  (TODO: wire to real endpoint); decorative grid overlay + orange glow blob

## App Layer

- `src/app/about/page.tsx` — RSC page, `Metadata` export, assembles feature components
  and an inline minimal footer (© 2026, Docs, Status, Privacy, Terms)
- `src/app/globals.css` — Added `@keyframes marquee` + `.animate-marquee` utility class

---

## Test Plan

- [ ] Visit `/about` — hero, ticker, platform grid, and waitlist form all render
- [ ] Observe ticker — scrolls continuously left without stutter or jump at the seam
- [ ] Submit the waitlist form — input clears and success state appears
- [ ] Resize to mobile — hero and platform grid reflow without overflow
title: feat(about): add About page

body:
## Summary

- Replaces the existing MUI/client-heavy listing detail page with a dark-themed
  two-column RSC layout built from the correct Figma frame (node 9721:5417)
- Page and all presentational sections are RSC; only the sidebar (save state) and
  preview tab bar (tab state) are `'use client'`
- Mock data wired directly in the RSC page — swap to real fetch by `id` param is
  a one-line change once the API is ready
- No MUI in new code — Tailwind CSS v4 throughout

---

## New Feature Slice (`src/features/listing/`)

- **`types/index.ts`** — `ListingReview`, `ListingResource`, `ListingCreator`,
  `ListingDetail` interfaces; reviews use product/company names, not individual users
- **`mock/mock-listing.ts`** — `MOCK_LISTING_DETAIL` (DataFlow Studio Pro); full data
  including 3 reviews (Nebula Vector DB, Prism UI Kit, Echo Realtime), 3 resources,
  creator profile (Binary Forge · @jammingOut · Singapore)
- **`listing-header.tsx`** — Breadcrumb nav, category + version badge pills, title,
  description, creator avatar + name, star rating, usage stats, tag pills
- **`listing-sidebar.tsx`** — `'use client'`; price + billing label, refund row with
  checkmark, white "Buy & Launch" CTA with arrow icon, white-bordered "Book setup call"
  CTA, Save/Share/Report action row; "Meet the creator" card below with avatar, handle,
  location, bio, tag chips, and "View Full Profile" link
- **`listing-about.tsx`** — "About this product" heading, multi-paragraph about text,
  2-column key features grid with orange-circle check icons, "View detailed usage guide" link
- **`listing-preview.tsx`** — `'use client'`; tab bar (Preview / Images / Documentation)
  with orange active underline; Preview shows video play button ("Watch product demo · 5 min overview");
  Documentation tab renders a quick-links list
- **`listing-resources.tsx`** — 3-card grid; `doc` / `slides` / `pdf` icon variants;
  external link per card
- **`listing-concierge.tsx`** — "// Concierge onboarding" banner with orange radial glow;
  right-aligned button pair: "Book 1:1 demo" (white filled) + "Talk to expert" (bordered)
- **`listing-reviews.tsx`** — Aggregate rating (4.7) + "based on N reviews" right-aligned;
  full-width stacked review rows; each row shows product name, green VERIFIED badge, star
  rating, review body, helpful count + timestamp

## App Layer

- `src/app/listing/[id]/page.tsx` — RSC; `grid-cols-[1fr_340px]` layout; sidebar sticky
  at `top-[93px]`; `Metadata` export; `<Suspense>` around client components;
  replaces old MUI client component entirely

---

## Test Plan

- [ ] Visit `/listing/dataflow-studio-pro` — header, two-column layout, all sections render
- [ ] Click Preview / Images / Documentation tabs — content switches, orange underline tracks
- [ ] Click the play button in Preview — no crash (video wiring is a future task)
- [ ] Click Save in sidebar — bookmark icon fills, label changes to "Saved"; click again to toggle
- [ ] Click "Buy & Launch" — no-op (not yet wired); no crash
- [ ] Click "View Full Profile" — navigates to `/account/jammingOut`
- [ ] Resize to a narrow viewport — grid stacks to single column without overflow
title: feat(listing): add Listing View page

body:
## Summary

- Implements the shared `Navbar` (MYT-57) and `Footer` components under `src/shared/ui/` for use across all four new AI-agent UI pages
- Replaces the legacy MUI `NavBar` (white, 64 px) app-wide — `providers.tsx` and `__root.tsx` now mount the new component; no feature flag needed
- No MUI in new code — Tailwind CSS v4 + shadcn/ui + Radix throughout; existing MUI usage untouched except for height constant updates

---

## New Files (`src/shared/`)

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `shared/ui/navbar.tsx` | Dark `#101010` fixed navbar matching Figma node `9723:6795` |
| `shared/ui/footer.tsx` | Dark footer with Company + Legal link columns and copyright line |
| `shared/ui/index.ts` | Barrel export — `export { Navbar, Footer }` |
| `shared/utils/mock-data.ts` | `MOCK_USER` constant; single source of truth, no prop drilling |

---

## Navbar (`shared/ui/navbar.tsx`)

- **Wordmark** — orange icon (`#ff9710`) rendered via CSS alpha-mask over `logo-white.png`; "Mythos" in white Unbounded font — replicates Figma's two-layer mask technique without extra assets
- **Search bar** — visual-only pill (Space Grotesk, `#9e978f`, `⌘ + K` badge in JetBrains Mono); click **or** `⌘ / Ctrl + K` opens the existing shadcn `CommandDialog`; no results wired
- **Nav links** — Discovery · Developers · Docs; `#d5d5d5` → white on hover; full keyboard `Tab` / `:focus-visible` ring support
- **Right icons** — `Sun` (theme toggle), `Bell` (notifications — dot rendered only when real notification state exists), empty orange avatar circle placeholder
- **Responsive** — full layout at `≥ 1280 px (xl)`; hamburger + mobile slide-down menu below `1280 px`; search collapses to icon-only below `768 px`

---

## Footer (`shared/ui/footer.tsx`)

- **Layout** — wordmark + tagline column on the left; **Company** (About, Blog, Careers, Contact) and **Legal** (Privacy Policy, Terms of Service, Cookie Policy, Licenses) columns on the right; collapses to a single column on mobile
- **Bottom bar** — `© {year} Mythos. All rights reserved.`; year computed at build time
- Same `#101010` / `border-white/[0.06]` design language as the navbar

---

## Infrastructure & Fixes

- `src/shared/ui/providers.tsx` — swapped `import NavBar from '@/composites/layout/nav-bar'` for `import { Navbar } from '@/shared/ui'`; removed the `navMounted` / `useEffect` SSR workaround (not needed for the new `'use client'` component)
- `src/routes/__root.tsx` — same NavBar → Navbar swap for the legacy TanStack Router path
- `src/app/account/layout.tsx` — all four `<Toolbar />` code paths updated from the implicit 64 px MUI default to `minHeight: '93px'`; Drawer `marginTop` and `height` updated from hardcoded `64px` to `93px`; loading/error states updated to match
- `src/app/account/[name]/page.tsx` — `pt-16` (64 px) → `pt-[93px]`; profile page bypasses the account layout Toolbar so it must carry its own navbar offset
- `src/app/account/wishlist/page.tsx` — `h-[calc(100vh-4rem)]` → `h-[calc(100vh-93px)]`
- `src/theme/theme.ts` — `navbarHeight` updated from `4` (rem) to `5.8125` (rem = 93 px); fixes all `h-[calc(100vh-${theme.navbarHeight}rem)]` usages in listings, billing, purchases, and earnings pages

---

## Test Plan

- [ ] Visit any page — dark `#101010` navbar renders with orange Mythos wordmark, search bar, nav links, Sun icon, Bell, and empty orange avatar circle
- [ ] Click the search bar or press `⌘ K` / `Ctrl K` — shadcn Command palette opens; type anything — "No results found." shown; `Esc` dismisses
- [ ] Tab through the navbar — all interactive elements (logo link, search button, three nav links, theme icon, bell, avatar) receive visible `:focus-visible` ring in correct order
- [ ] Resize browser below 1280 px — nav links and icons collapse; hamburger icon appears; click to open slide-down menu with nav links + icons
- [ ] Resize below 768 px — search bar hides; search icon appears beside hamburger; click search icon — Command palette opens
- [ ] Scroll to bottom of any page — footer renders with Company and Legal columns, wordmark, and copyright line
- [ ] Visit `/account/alexmorgan` — no white gap between navbar and dark page content; content starts flush below the 93 px navbar
- [ ] Visit `/account/listings` (or any sidebar account page) — sidebar and content remain correctly positioned below the navbar with no double offset
- [ ] Verify Bell has no orange dot in default state
title: feat(shared-ui): add shared Navbar and Footer across all pages [MYT-57]

body:
## Summary

- **2-step create listing wizard** at `/listing/create` (`src/features/listing/`): setup (product type, connection) → build & publish (details, theme, blocks, media, pricing, live preview).
- **Manage Listings hub** at `/account/listings` with Figma-aligned header, tabs, and listing cards.
- **Creator studio sidebar** on account and listing-create routes when `rolePreference === 'creator'`.
- **Figma alignment:** layout presets (sidebar / compact / centered), Trust Anchor CTA, wizard footer copy, dev category fallback, `/backend-api` proxy for local API.
- **Playwright:** `e2e/create-listing-wizard.spec.ts`, `e2e/figma-mvp-listing-alignment.spec.ts`, and helpers.

**Stacks on #65** (`feat/dark-tokens-next`) for dark theme + hydration fixes.

## Related tickets

- [MT-295](https://mythosplatform.atlassian.net/browse/MT-295)
- [MT-296](https://mythosplatform.atlassian.net/browse/MT-296)
- [MT-291](https://mythosplatform.atlassian.net/browse/MT-291)
- [MT-297](https://mythosplatform.atlassian.net/browse/MT-297)

## Test plan

- [ ] `npm run build`
- [ ] `npm run dev` → `/listing/create` (digital + hosted paths) and `/account/listings`
- [ ] `npx playwright test e2e/create-listing-wizard.spec.ts e2e/figma-mvp-listing-alignment.spec.ts` (API on `:5000` optional; categories use dev fallback)
title: feat(listing): creator listing flow — 2-step wizard and manage listings hub

body:
## Summary
- Port forced-dark shadcn tokens to globals.css and align legacy styles.
- Add RootErrorBoundary and MUI CssBaseline aligned with dark Figma tokens.
- Fix NavBar hydration via AppRouterCacheProvider (enableCssLayer).

## Test plan
- [x] npm run build
- [x] next dev — spot-check dark UI and NavBar (no hydration warnings)
title: feat(ui): Figma dark tokens and root error boundary (Next)

body:
## Summary

- Implements the User Profile (MT-298) and Creator Profile (MT-299) pages
  under a single `/account/[name]` route, branching layout by `rolePreference`
- All data served by Next.js Route Handlers under `/api/mock/` — no backend
  branch required; swap to real endpoints is a URL prefix change only
- No MUI in new code — Tailwind CSS v4 + shadcn/ui + Radix throughout

---

## New Mock API Routes (`src/app/api/mock/`)

| Route | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `GET /api/mock/profile/name/[name]` | Profile lookup by username; returns consumer or creator shape |
| `GET /api/mock/users/[id]/stats` | Five user stat counters (reviews, following, collections, wishlist, installs) |
| `GET /api/mock/users/[id]/library` | Paginated installed products with status |
| `GET /api/mock/users/[id]/collections` | User's named collections |
| `GET /api/mock/users/[id]/activity` | Five most recent activity events |
| `GET /api/mock/users/[id]/reviews` | User's written reviews with helpful counts |
| `GET /api/mock/creators/[id]/stats` | Four creator stat counters (followers, products, installs, rating) |
| `GET /api/mock/creators/[id]/products` | Creator's published listings grid |
| `GET /api/mock/creators/[id]/reviews` | Reviews received by creator |
| `GET /api/mock/creators/[id]/featured` | Featured release card detail |

---

## New React Query Hooks (`src/hooks/profile/`)

- `use-user-stats` — user + creator stats endpoints
- `use-user-library` — library with `status` field
- `use-user-collections` — collections list
- `use-user-activity` — recent activity with `action_type`
- `use-user-reviews` — written reviews, maps snake_case → camelCase
- `use-creator-products` — creator product grid
- `use-creator-reviews` — creator reviews, maps snake_case → camelCase
- `use-creator-featured` — featured release data

---

## User Profile (MT-298)

- **Header** — 160×160 avatar, display name, bio (2 lines), joined date + location
  separated by `·`, share button; edit pencil button rendered only for profile owner
- **Stats bar** — five equal-width tiles with icons: Reviews · Following ·
  Collections · Wishlist · Installs
- **Sticky section nav** — frosted-glass bar; active tab has 2px orange underline
  with glow; smooth-scrolls to anchored sections
- **Library section** — grid/list view toggle (wired); filter button; 3-column
  product card grid; each card shows status badge: ● Active (green) /
  ● Installed (muted) / ● Inactive (dimmed)
- **Reviews section** — VERIFIED badge, star rating, 2-line body, helpful count
  + timestamp
- **Collections section** — 3-column grid; amber border appears on hover only
- **Recent activity sidebar** — 320px panel; icons vary by action type:
  Download (purchased) · MessageSquare (reviewed) · Heart (saved)

---

## Creator Profile (MT-299)

- **Header** — 160×160 avatar, creator name, VERIFIED CREATOR badge (green),
  bio, meta row (founded year · location · website link), share button;
  Follow/Following toggle button
- **Stats bar** — four tiles: Followers · Products · Installs · Rating
  (displayed to 2 decimal places)
- **Sticky section nav** — same pattern as user profile; tabs: Overview ·
  Products · Reviews · About
- **Featured Release card** — dark 320px showcase area with product name,
  technical tagline, and Watch Demo button; bottom panel has description,
  feature tag chips, Install button, price, and star rating
- **All Products grid** — 3-column; FEATURED chip on designated product;
  amber border appears on hover only (not permanently on featured card)
- **Reviews section** — aggregate rating histogram (score + 5 stars + bar
  chart) above individual review items with initial avatar, verified check,
  "on [Product] · [time]" meta row, and star rating
- **About section** — description, MISSION sub-label, TEAM sub-label;
  hidden from section nav if `user.about` is unpopulated
- **Interests sidebar** — wrapping tag chips from `user.interests`

---

## Infrastructure & Fixes

- `src/features/profile/types/index.ts` — canonical type definitions extracted
  from page; includes `ActivityItem.actionType`, `LibraryItem.status`
- `src/app/account/layout.tsx` — detects profile pages by pathname and returns
  `<>{children}</>` before any MUI renders, eliminating SSR hydration mismatch
- `src/proxy.ts` — removed blanket `/account` from private prefixes; public
  profile routes are now accessible without authentication
- `src/api/profile-service.ts` — `getProfileByName` updated to call
  `/api/mock/profile/name/[name]`

---

## Test Plan

- [ ] Visit `/account/alexmorgan` — user profile renders with all five sections
      and activity sidebar
- [ ] Visit `/account/creator` — creator profile renders with featured release,
      products grid, histogram, and interests sidebar
- [ ] Hover over a collection card — amber border appears; remove mouse — border
      disappears
- [ ] Hover over a creator product card — same amber hover behaviour
- [ ] Click grid/list toggle on library section — layout switches
- [ ] Click Follow on creator profile — button toggles to "Following" state;
      click again to unfollow
- [ ] Sign in as the mock user and visit their own profile — edit pencil button
      appears beside the share button
- [ ] Verify activity sidebar icons: Download for purchased, MessageSquare for
      reviewed, Heart for saved
title: feat: add user and creator profile pages (MT-298, MT-299)

body:
## Summary

- Force the design system dark palette by adding `class="dark"` on `<html>` and updating `.dark` shadcn CSS variables in `src/styles/index.css` (page/card contrast, amber primary + hover, near-black primary-foreground, 8% white borders, tuned ring/input).
- Map `--color-primary-hover` in `@theme inline` and use `primary-hover` on the default Badge hover state.
- Add `RootErrorBoundary` (`src/components/root-error-boundary.tsx`) and wrap the app tree in `src/main.tsx` so render failures show a safe fallback (with extra detail in dev).

## Notes

- MUI `CssBaseline` + the light MUI theme can still set a light `body` background; full-screen dark chrome may need a follow-up to align MUI with the same tokens.

## Test plan

- [ ] `npm run build` succeeds locally.
- [ ] Spot-check screens that use shadcn `Button` / `Badge` / `Input` / `Card` for token-driven colors under `html.dark`.
- [ ] In dev, force a throw inside the tree and confirm the error boundary UI; in a production build, confirm generic copy only.
title: feat(ui): align forced-dark tokens with Figma and add root error boundary

body:
## Summary

- Fixes a copy-paste bug where Wallet and Transaction sidebar items both navigate to `/account/billing`, causing both to highlight as active simultaneously
- Guards `/signup/onboarding` from unauthenticated access — the route was missing from `PRIVATE_PREFIXES` in the proxy, leaving it publicly accessible
- Removes dead code in the nav bar: `getCreatorTools()` was called twice per render, and `isError`/`profileError` were destructured but never used

## Key files changed

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/composites/layout/side-bar.tsx` | Fix duplicate `/account/billing` path on Wallet item |
| `src/proxy.ts` | Add `/signup/onboarding` to `PRIVATE_PREFIXES` |
| `src/composites/layout/nav-bar.tsx` | Assign `getCreatorTools()` to a const before render; remove dead `isError`/`profileError` destructuring |

## Test plan

- [ ] `npm run build` — clean build, no TS errors, no warnings
- [ ] Wallet and Transaction sidebar items navigate to different routes and only the correct one highlights as active
- [ ] Visiting `/signup/onboarding` while logged out redirects to `/login`
- [ ] Visiting `/signup/onboarding` while fully registered redirects to `/discovery`

## What's next

Auth consolidation follows in `fix/consolidate-auth` — the `requiresSignup` JWT cast in `proxy.ts` will be properly typed there.
title: fix(next): sidebar route, onboarding auth guard, and nav-bar dead code

body: ## Summary - Adds HTTP security response headers to all routes via `next.config.ts` — the app currently ships no security headers, a meaningful gap for a platform handling payments and auth - Covers the highest-impact zero-configuration wins: clickjacking prevention, MIME sniffing, referrer control, and permissions scoping - Adds `images.remotePatterns` to prevent `next/image` build failures as `` tags are migrated to `` - CSP is intentionally deferred — it requires auditing all inline scripts, third-party domains (Google OAuth, MUI emotion), and is better tackled standalone to avoid breaking changes ## Key files changed | File | Change | |---|---| | `next.config.ts` | Add `headers()` export with `X-Frame-Options`, `X-Content-Type-Options`, `Referrer-Policy`, `Permissions-Policy` applied to all routes | | `next.config.ts` | Add `images.remotePatterns` for external image domains | ## Test plan - [ ] `npm run build` — clean build, no TS errors, no warnings - [ ] `curl -I https:///` — response includes all four headers - [ ] Opening the app in a frame (`
title: feat(security): add HTTP response headers and image remote patterns

- Installs Next.js 16 (App Router) alongside the existing Vite/React SPA — build is clean, no warnings

## What's next

body:
## Summary

Fixes all `npm run build` errors after migrating from TanStack Router to Next.js.

## Changes

### Navigation Patterns
- Replaced `Route.useSearch()` with `useSearchParams()` (from `next/navigation`)
- Replaced `searchParams.prop` with `searchParams.get('prop')`
- Replaced `navigate({ to: '...', search: {...} })` with `router.push()`
- Fixed stale `navigate` references in `useEffect` dependency arrays (`login`, `reset/*` pages)

### Suspense Boundaries
Next.js requires `useSearchParams()` to be wrapped in a `<Suspense>` boundary. Added wrappers on all affected pages: `account`, `account/listings`, `verify`, `reset/password`, `reset/verifyotp`, `discovery`, `discovery/all`, `listing/create`, `listing/create/bundle`

### SSR / DOMMatrix Fixes
`pdfjs-dist` executes `let bz = new DOMMatrix` at module top-level, which throws in Node.js during static page generation. Fixed by making browser-only components dynamic with `ssr: false`: `Step3Content`, `Step4Details`, `Step1Bundle`, `Step2Bundle`, `PreviewModal`.

All 28 pages build and prerender successfully.
title: fix: migrate TanStack Router patterns to Next.js navigation

body:
Tags: @M-Sharan-Balaji @dngvmnh @AmirWVR 

Generated with:
1. https://github.com/github/spec-kit
2. Claude Code

These additions help the AI tools to narrow down the task to not be too generic and can target specific files/flows. This is **only an initialization. Rest assured, the constitutions, prompts, etc. will be updated periodically during the next development process**.

---

## **⚠️ Do note that there is a big possibility we migrate to Next.js. It still uses React with almost instant migration (<1 day) since Next.js uses React too ⚠️**

---

### Summary

- **Project Constitution** (`CLAUDE.md`, `.specify/memory/constitution.md`, `.windsurf/rules/`, `.cursor/rules/`): Defines the agreed architecture, coding standards, and tech decisions for all AI agents working in this repo. Covers vertical feature slices, state management layers, Tailwind-only styling, TypeScript strict mode, MUI deprecation, and testing strategy.
- **Next.js migration signal**: Constitution bakes in the App Router architecture (RSC vs client component guidance, `app/` routing layer, updated state/performance patterns) ahead of the expected migration. New features should be written against these conventions now.
- **Speckit workflow kit** (`.claude/skills/`, `.cursor/skills/`, `.windsurf/workflows/`, `.specify/`): Installs a structured spec-to-code workflow — `specify → clarify → plan → tasks → implement → checklist`. Any agent (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf) can run these workflows against a feature description to produce consistent, constitution-aligned output.

### What this changes for developers

This PR is **non-functional** — zero changes to app runtime behaviour. It adds three things:

1. A single source of truth for architectural decisions so reviewers and AI agents agree on what "correct" looks like.
4. A workflow that turns a feature brief into a spec, implementation plan, and task list before any code is written.
5. Propagation of those rules to all four agentic tool directories so Windsurf, Cursor, Claude Code, and Specify all operate from the same constitution.

### Notes

The Next.js migration is not in scope for this PR. When it begins, the `app/` folder structure and RSC/client component split described in the constitution are the target state. Feature work in the interim should avoid patterns that would conflict with that migration (e.g., TanStack Router-specific APIs, heavy Vite-only optimisations).
title: feat(spec): Initialize front-end spec-kit

## Summary

Adjusts the creator dashboard so the three sales/earnings metric tiles stay in a single horizontal row on small screens and align evenly, with typography and spacing scaled down on narrow viewports.

## Changes

- **Tiles**: `flex: 1 1 0`, `minWidth: 0`, and `boxSizing: border-box` so the three boxes share width predictably in a row.
- **Spacing**: responsive padding, gap, and tile min-height (`xs` tighter than `sm+`).
- **Type**: smaller value, label, and "as of" text on `xs`.
- **Layout**: metrics area uses a horizontal flex row with `nowrap` instead of the previous grid-based layout.

body:
**Summary**
This PR fixes a publish gating issue on edit listing flow where reopened drafts could not be published unless the user changed visibility or quantity first.

**Cause**
Publish button disable logic required hasListingChanges for all cases.
Reopened draft state equals stored draft state, so hasListingChanges is false.
Result: publish button stayed disabled until any field was modified.

**Changes**
Updated publish disable condition:
Require hasListingChanges only for non-draft listings.
Allow valid drafts to publish immediately without unrelated edits.
Kept existing validation checks intact (isStep4Disabled, step completion, title requirement).
title: fix(listing/edit): allow publishing reopened drafts without forced field toggles

body:
## Landing Page Restructure

Refactored the landing page from a monolithic component into a clean, modular architecture.
All components are now theme-token compliant with no hard-coded hex values.

Actual lay-out isn't ready as there are still many changes that need to be made regarding the use of certain assets as well as placement of certain assets.

### Structure

`index.tsx` now mounts a single `<LandingPage />` component from `page.tsx`, which
composes the following sections in order:

| Component | File | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `<Header />` | `header.tsx` | Extracted from `hero.tsx` — logo + auth buttons |
| `<Hero />` | `hero.tsx` | Refactored — removed embedded header |
| `<LandingCarousel />` | `landing-carousel.tsx` | New — auto-scrolling carousel with `<Banner />` sub-component using Embla |
| `<WhyChooseUs />` | `why-choose-us.tsx` | Replaces `trending-now.tsx` — interactive accordion + image panel |
| `<HowItWorks />` | `how-it-works.tsx` | Replaces `about.tsx` — step cards with screenshots |
| `<HeroFooter />` | `hero-footer.tsx` | Replaces bottom of `call-to-action.tsx` — CTA with floating product chips |
| `<Footer />` | `Footer.tsx` | Unchanged |

### Changes

- Extracted `<Header />` from `hero.tsx` into its own file
- Created shared `<LandingCard />` primitive in `landing-card.tsx`
- Replaced all hard-coded hex values with MUI theme tokens (`theme.palette.*`)
  and Tailwind CSS variables (`bg-primary`, `text-foreground`, etc.)
- `index.tsx` reduced to routing logic only (~20 lines)
- Added `embla-carousel-autoplay` dependency for carousel auto-scroll

### Files Added
- `src/routes/page.tsx`
- `src/composites/landing-page/header.tsx`
- `src/composites/landing-page/landing-card.tsx`
- `src/composites/landing-page/landing-carousel.tsx`
- `src/composites/landing-page/why-choose-us.tsx`
- `src/composites/landing-page/how-it-works.tsx`
- `src/composites/landing-page/hero-footer.tsx`

### Files Modified
- `src/routes/index.tsx`
- `src/composites/landing-page/hero.tsx`

### Files Superseded (kept, no longer mounted)
- `src/composites/landing-page/about.tsx`
- `src/composites/landing-page/trending-now.tsx`
- `src/composites/landing-page/call-to-action.tsx`
- `src/composites/landing-page/landing-page-bar.tsx`
title: refactor(mobile): Restructure responsive landing page layout

body: # PR: Implement Events & Bookings Schedule Page Mentioning @M-Sharan-Balaji , @dngvmnh , @AmirWVR ## Summary This PR implements a new dedicated Schedule page at `/account/schedule` that allows users to view and manage their events from both buyer (attendee) and creator (host) perspectives. The page includes filtering capabilities, event grouping by time periods, and reschedule functionality for creators. --- ⚠️ Don't forget to turn on your [BE locally](https://github.com/Mythoswork/backend/tree/f/add-schedule-endpoint) and use branch [`f/add-schedule-endpoint`](https://github.com/Mythoswork/backend/tree/f/add-schedule-endpoint) ⚠️ ## Changes ### New Files 1. **`src/routes/account/schedule.tsx`** - Main schedule page component - Host/Attend perspective toggle - Filter tabs (All, Upcoming, Needs Action, Completed) - Events grouped by: Today, Upcoming Week, Upcoming Month - Toast notifications for success/error feedback 2. **`src/composites/schedule/event-card.tsx`** - Reusable event card component with date badge - Role-based action buttons: - **Host view**: Reschedule + Start Call/Event buttons - **Attend view**: Join Call button - Time-based button states (before/during/after event) - Fully responsive layout (stacks on mobile) 3. **`src/composites/schedule/reschedule-modal.tsx`** - Modal for creators to reschedule events - Date/time pickers for new proposed time - Warning about attendee notification - Responsive design with mobile-optimized layout ### Modified Files 4. **`src/api/dashboard-service.ts`** - Added `ScheduleEvent` interface - Added `ScheduleResponse` interface - Added `fetchSchedule()` function with query params: - `filter`: `all` | `upcoming` | `needs_action` | `completed` - `perspective`: `all` | `host` | `attend` 5. **`src/composites/layout/side-bar.tsx`** - Added "Schedule" menu item with Calendar icon - Positioned between "Purchase" and "Wallet" 6. **`src/routes/discovery/index.tsx`** - Added "Schedule" to mobile sidebar links --- ## Features ### Host/Attend Toggle - **Host mode**: Shows events where user is the creator - **Attend mode**: Shows events where user is the buyer - Clean toggle UI with active state styling ### Filtering - **All**: Shows all events for selected perspective - **Upcoming**: Future events only - **Needs Action**: Events requiring attention (uses `needsAction` field) - **Completed**: Past or fulfilled events ### Event Grouping Events are automatically grouped by the backend into: - **Today**: Events happening today - **Upcoming Week**: Events within next 7 days - **Upcoming Month**: Events within next 30 days ### Reschedule Functionality (Creator Only) - Click "Reschedule" button to open modal - Select new start and end times - Simplified flow: directly updates event time (no approval workflow) - Attendees receive automatic notification ### Responsive Design - Mobile-first approach with breakpoints (xs, sm, md) - Event cards stack vertically on mobile - Host/Attend toggle stacks on mobile - Filter tabs wrap naturally - Optimized padding and spacing for all screen sizes --- ## API Integration ### Endpoint **GET** `/api/dashboard/schedule` ### Query Parameters - `filter`: Filter events by status - `perspective`: Filter by user role (host/attend/all) ### Response Structure ```typescript { success: true, schedule: { today: ScheduleEvent[], upcoming_week: ScheduleEvent[], upcoming_month: ScheduleEvent[] }, filter: "all" | "upcoming" | "needs_action" | "completed", perspective: "all" | "host" | "attend" } ``` ### ScheduleEvent Fields - `orderId`, `listingId`, `listingTitle` - `role`: `"buyer"` | `"creator"` - `creatorName`, `buyerName` - `eventStart`, `eventEnd` (ISO timestamps) - `meetLink`, `eventLink` - `status`: `"paid"` | `"fulfilled"` - `needsAction`: boolean - `attendeeCount`: number --- ## How to Test ### Prerequisites - Backend API running with `/api/dashboard/schedule` endpoint - Test account with both buyer and creator events - Events in different time periods (today, this week, this month) ### Test Steps #### 1. Navigation - [ ] Log in to the application - [ ] Click "Schedule" in the sidebar - [ ] Verify route navigates to `/account/schedule` - [ ] Verify "Schedule" menu item is highlighted in sidebar #### 2. Host/Attend Toggle - [ ] Page loads with "Attend" view by default - [ ] Click "Host" button - [ ] Verify button has white background (active state) - [ ] Verify events list updates to show creator events only - [ ] Verify event cards show "with [buyer name]" or "with X buyers" - [ ] Click "Attend" button - [ ] Verify button has white background (active state) - [ ] Verify events list updates to show buyer events only - [ ] Verify event cards show "with [creator name]" #### 3. Filter Tabs - [ ] Click "All" tab - verify all events display - [ ] Click "Upcoming" tab - verify only future events display - [ ] Click "Needs Action" tab - verify only events with `needsAction=true` display - [ ] Click "Completed" tab - verify only past/fulfilled events display - [ ] Verify active tab has black background #### 4. Event Grouping - [ ] Verify events are grouped under correct headers: - [ ] "Today" section appears if events exist today - [ ] "Upcoming Week" section appears if events exist within 7 days - [ ] "Upcoming Month" section appears if events exist within 30 days - [ ] Verify sections only appear when they have events #### 5. Event Card Display - [ ] Verify each event card shows: - [ ] Date badge (month + day) - [ ] Event title (truncated if too long) - [ ] Time range in local timezone - [ ] Participant name ("with [name]") #### 6. Host View Actions Switch to "Host" perspective and test: - [ ] **Before Event**: - [ ] "Reschedule" button is visible and enabled - [ ] "Start Call/Event" button is visible but disabled - [ ] **During Event** (adjust system time or wait for event time): - [ ] "Reschedule" button is visible - [ ] "Start Call/Event" button is enabled - [ ] Click "Start Call" - verify meetLink opens in new tab - [ ] **After Event**: - [ ] Both buttons show "Completed" badge - [ ] No action buttons are clickable #### 7. Attend View Actions Switch to "Attend" perspective and test: - [ ] **Before Event**: - [ ] Shows "Upcoming" badge (no action button) - [ ] **During Event**: - [ ] "Join Call" button appears and is enabled - [ ] Click "Join Call" - verify meetLink opens in new tab - [ ] **After Event**: - [ ] Shows "Completed" badge #### 8. Reschedule Functionality (Host Only) - [ ] Switch to "Host" view - [ ] Click "Reschedule" button on any event - [ ] Verify modal opens with: - [ ] Event title - [ ] Current time displayed - [ ] Start time picker - [ ] End time picker - [ ] Warning message about attendee notification - [ ] Select new start time - [ ] Select new end time (should not allow time before start) - [ ] Click "Confirm Reschedule" - [ ] Verify success toast appears - [ ] Verify modal closes - [ ] Verify event list refreshes with new time - [ ] Click "Reschedule" again and click "Cancel" - [ ] Verify modal closes without changes --- ## Screenshots _Add screenshots showing:_ - Desktop view (Host and Attend modes) **please ignore the sidebar, the mobile view should be under development somewhere else** --- ## Notes - All filtering and grouping is handled by the backend - no client-side processing - Backend must return pre-grouped events in the response - Reschedule uses existing event update API (e.g., `PUT /api/listings/event/{listingId}`) - No approval workflow for reschedules in this MVP (direct update) - TanStack Router will auto-generate route types on dev server start
title: feat(schedule): Add schedule page

body:
**Summary**
This change updates the login page Google handler to support the new backend signup signal. When Google login returns requiresSignup, the client verifies Google profile data, stores onboarding prefill in session storage, and redirects to signup onboarding without surfacing invalid-credentials messaging.

**Problem**
Login page treated all unsuccessful Google login responses as hard errors, producing invalid credentials messaging for users who should be routed to signup.

**What changed**

- Parse Google login response body once.
- Handle requiresSignup response from Google login endpoint.
- On requiresSignup:
- call Google token verification endpoint
- save oauth_provider, oauth_email, oauth_name in session storage
- navigate to signup onboarding
- Keep existing success login path unchanged.
- Keep existing non-signup failure handling for true error scenarios.
- Behavior after change

- Existing Google user: logs in as before.
- New Google user: automatically routed into manual signup flow.
title: fix: handle signup redirection with Google account

body:
## Login & Onboarding UI — Review Fixes

### Bug Fixes
- **Broken JSX structure** (onboarding Step 0): Fixed misaligned closing `</Box>` tags in the Google SSO + "or sign up with email" divider section — the divider was incorrectly nested inside the SSO wrapper box
- **`mu: 0` typo**: Changed to `mt: 0` on the subtitle Typography (was silently ignored by MUI)
- **`lineHeight: 0.1`** on subtitle: Changed to `1.4` — prevented text from rendering on top of itself if it wraps
- **`lineHeight: 0.5`** on headings: Changed to `1.2` in both `login/index.tsx` and `onboarding.tsx`

### Style & Convention Fixes
- Replaced `"#F00"` with `"error.main"` for the username warning text
- Replaced `"#000"` / `"#FFFFFF"` on Continue/Finish/Back buttons with theme tokens: `"secondary.main"`, `"primary.contrastText"`, `"text.disabled"`
- Replaced `bgcolor: "#FFF"` on the nav bar with `"background.paper"`
- Removed `minHeight: "776.75px"` magic number — card height is now content-driven
- Replaced `gap: 90` on the fixed nav bar with a proper inner container (`width: "70%", maxWidth: "900px"`) that aligns buttons with the interests section
- Changed `width: 382` on "Agree & Continue" button to `width: "100%"`
- Changed `pt: "33px"` to `pt: 4` for MUI spacing consistency
- Changed `mb: "1.4375rem"` to `mb: 2` — removes unnecessary precision
- Removed `// or "smooth"` leftover comment from scroll behaviour

### UX Improvements
- **Fixed bottom nav bar overlap**: Added `pb` equal to the nav bar height (`79px`) to the page container when `activeStep > 0`, preventing the last item on each step from being hidden behind the fixed bar
- **Nav bar button alignment**: Back and Continue/Finish buttons now align with the left and right edges of the "Select your interests" grid. Added `gap: 2` to prevent overlap on narrow screens
- **Password hint timing**: Added `passwordTouched` state — the "Enter at least 8 characters" hint now only appears after the user has interacted with the password field, not on page load
- **Mobile edge fix** (login page): Added `px: { xs: 2, sm: 0 }` to the card wrapper so the login box no longer hits the phone screen edges on mobile

### Login Page Restyling
- Page background updated to `#faf8f4` (warm off-white, matching app default)
- Card aligned to top of page instead of vertically centered
- "Welcome back" heading reduced to `1.75rem` with `lineHeight: 1.2`
- Plain `Divider` replaced with a styled "or sign in with email" separator (matching onboarding)
- Email/Password field labels reduced in font weight for a lighter look
- Email placeholder updated to `your@email.com`
- Login button restyled: grey by default, orange on hover
- "Sign up here" link colour updated to `primary.main`
title: refactor(UI): Onboarding page update

body:
**Login & Onboarding UI Improvements**
_Login (src/routes/login/index.tsx)_

1. Updated page background color to match the app's default warm off-white (#faf8f4)
2. Moved the login card to align top instead of center, with controlled top padding for better visual placement
3. Reduced "Welcome back" heading font size to 1.75rem
4. Replaced the plain Divider with a styled "or sign in with email" separator to match the onboarding page design
5. Reduced label font weight for Email and Password fields to match onboarding style
6. Updated email placeholder text to your@email.com
7. Restyled the Login button — now grey by default with an orange hover state
8. Updated the "Sign up here" link color to primary.main (orange)

_Onboarding (src/routes/signup/onboarding.tsx)_

1. Added a useEffect to scroll to the top of the page on each step transition
2. Replaced the single heading "Join our community" with a title + subtitle layout: "Create your Mythos account" and "Join creators and learners in one place"
3. Replaced the Divider with a styled "or sign up with email" separator, consistent with the login page
4. Added "Already have an account? Log in" link at the bottom of Step 0
5. Updated the legal agreement text to reference Terms of Service and Privacy Policy directly (removed separate Creator Agreement link)
6. Restyled the Step 0 card: adjusted padding, increased max width to 608px, added a min height
7. Reduced field label font weights for a lighter look
8. Updated the stepper: narrowed to 40% width, added orange active/completed connector and icon colors
9. Moved the Back/Continue/Finish navigation bar to a fixed bottom bar for better UX across all steps
10. Restyled nav buttons to use black filled buttons with uppercase text
11. Updated the "Usernames cannot be changed" warning to red
12. Changed background color to background.default (warm off-white)
13. Various spacing and layout refinements across all three steps
title: Onboarding page update
fix(timezone): Fix timezone mismatch

body:
One of my first tasks in Mythos was to revamp the Mobile View. In order to do that, I figured the first thing to do would be to edit the frontend such that each file didn't use hard-coded values, but instead the presets from theme.css. 

Some of the values within theme.css were also changed in order to match the actual website, as there were some miscolorations when running it locally upon changing the hard-coded values for the first time.
title: refactor: Revamp and replace hard-coded colours

body: # Spec: Fix XSS Vulnerabilities from `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` **Priority**: CRITICAL — Security **Effort**: Small (2–4 hours) **Type**: Security Bug --- ## Background React deliberately makes you write `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` to remind you that injecting raw HTML is dangerous. When you inject a string that contains HTML tags, the browser parses and executes it as real HTML — including `
title: fix: sanitize HTML in dangerouslySetInnerHTML to prevent XSS

body:
# Spec: Move Hardcoded Google Client ID to Environment Variable

**Priority**: CRITICAL — Security
**Effort**: Very Small (30 minutes)
**Type**: Security / Configuration

---

## Background

A Google OAuth Client ID is a credential that scopes what application is allowed to request Google sign-in. While it is less sensitive than a private key, hardcoding it in source code causes two real problems:

1. **It's committed to git history permanently.** Even if you change it later, the old value remains in every commit before the change. Anyone with git access can see it.
2. **Different environments need different values.** Production, staging, and local development should each use their own OAuth client registrations, with different allowed redirect URIs. A hardcoded value in source forces all environments to share one registration.

---

## The Problem

**File**: `src/main.tsx`, line 20

```typescript
const GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID = "196882362205-i2pbph8k2o0172g0pvn3k6teehmdpt51.apps.googleusercontent.com";
```

This value is hardcoded directly in source and committed to the repository.

---

## What to Fix

### Step 1 — Create environment variable files

Create `.env.local` in the project root (this file should already be in `.gitignore`):

```
VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=196882362205-i2pbph8k2o0172g0pvn3k6teehmdpt51.apps.googleusercontent.com
```

Create `.env.example` in the project root (this file IS committed — it serves as documentation):

```
# Google OAuth Client ID
# Get this from Google Cloud Console > APIs & Services > Credentials
VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=your-google-client-id-here
```

### Step 2 — Update `src/main.tsx`

```typescript
// Before (line 20):
const GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID = "196882362205-i2pbph8k2o0172g0pvn3k6teehmdpt51.apps.googleusercontent.com";

// After:
const GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID = import.meta.env.VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID;
```

Vite exposes env variables prefixed with `VITE_` via `import.meta.env`. No additional packages are needed.

### Step 3 — Confirm `.gitignore` covers `.env.local`

Check that `.gitignore` already includes `.env.local`. Most Vite project templates include it. If not, add it:

```
# .gitignore
.env.local
.env.*.local
```

---

## Deployment Considerations

Every deployment environment (Vercel, Fly.io, Render, etc.) needs `VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` set as an environment variable in the platform's dashboard. Vite bakes env variables into the bundle at build time, so they must be set before running `npm run build`.

Coordinate with whoever manages deployments to ensure this variable is set in:
- Local development (`.env.local`)
- CI/CD pipeline (as a secret/env var in the pipeline config)
- Production deployment platform

---

## Scope Boundaries — What NOT to Change

- Do not rotate or change the Client ID value itself as part of this spec — that is a separate security decision for whoever manages the Google Cloud project.
- Do not add runtime validation for the env variable (e.g., throwing if it's undefined). That is acceptable future work but out of scope here.
- Do not move any other hardcoded value as part of this PR. Only the Google Client ID.

---

## How to Verify

1. `import.meta.env.VITE_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` resolves to the correct string at runtime (check in browser devtools: `window.__vite_env` is not exposed, but Google OAuth will succeed if it's correct).
2. Search for the client ID string `196882362205` in `src/` — zero results.
3. The string `196882362205` should only appear in `.env.local` (which is not committed).
4. `.env.example` is committed and contains a placeholder, not the real value.
5. Google Sign-In still works in development after the change.
title: fix(`.env`): Move hardcoded Google Client ID to `.env`
